examples.title: Algorithm: Water Jug Problem
examples.description: Solving the Water Jug Problem using a breadth-first search algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM WaterJugProblem
INPUT: Initial state (0, 0), Goal state (2, x)
OUTPUT: Sequence of actions to reach goal state

BEGIN
 Create a queue to hold states to be explored
 Create a set to hold visited states
 Add initial state to queue and visited set
 WHILE queue is not empty
 Dequeue a state
 IF state is goal state
 RETURN sequence of actions
 ELSE
 Generate next states by applying actions (fill, empty, pour)
 FOR each next state
 IF next state has not been visited
 Add next state to queue and visited set
 Record sequence of actions
 RETURN failure
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates a breadth-first search algorithm to solve the Water Jug Problem. It explores all possible states by applying actions (fill, empty, pour) and keeps track of visited states to avoid infinite loops.

examples.title: Python: Water Jug Problem
examples.description: Solving the Water Jug Problem using a breadth-first search algorithm in Python
examples.explanation: This Python code implements a breadth-first search algorithm to solve the Water Jug Problem. It uses a queue to hold states to be explored and a set to keep track of visited states.

examples.title: Java: 8-Puzzle Problem
examples.description: Solving the 8-Puzzle Problem using a breadth-first search algorithm in Java
examples.explanation: This Java code implements a breadth-first search algorithm to solve the 8-Puzzle Problem. It uses a queue to hold boards to be explored and checks if the current board is the goal board.
